mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 8.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations and consumer spending acceleration that lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply expansion, reducing input cost volatility while boosting aggregate revenue expectations, which compresses equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate remains near zero, real rates turn negative, forcing institutional investors to chase yield in equities and generating sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: Equity risk premiums expand rather than contract when real rates are negative, causing SPX 20-day returns to be statistically indistinguishable from zero or negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer credit balances reflect immediate household spending strength, which translates directly into higher quarterly earnings revisions for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents, driving positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 95.0 index points, factory capacity utilization is fully deployed, signaling robust order books and pricing power for industrial and materials sector equities, which lifts the broader index over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO breaks above 95.0 due to supply chain bottlenecks or margin compression overriding revenue growth.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle, which validates earnings growth assumptions and reduces equity volatility, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when payroll growth exceeds 200,000 but unemployment remains sticky above 7.5%, indicating structural labor market disconnects.

mechanism_narrative: During confirmed GDP contractions, subsequent quarterly GDP growth accelerates as base effects and policy stimulus take hold, causing mean-reversion trading strategies to generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX continues to decline for more than 10 consecutive trading days following a confirmed GDP contraction, invalidating the mean-reversion premise.
